Z"d@dA
Z#dBdC
Seta!
A simple set class.
    This class was originally used to deal with sets being missing in
    ancient versions of python, but dnspython will continue to use it
    as these sets are based on lists and are thus indexable, and this
    ability is widely used in dnspython applications.
    
itemsNc
zaInitialize the set.
        *items*, an iterable or ``None``, the initial set of items.
        N)
odictr
add)
selfr
item
+/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/dns/set.py
__init__&
Set.__init__c
dns.set.Set(%s))
repr
listr
keys
__repr__1
Set.__repr__c
z Add an item to the set.
        N
Set.addc
z%Remove an item from the set.
        N)
KeyError
ValueErrorr
remove;
Set.removec
z0Remove an item from the set if present.
        N)
popr
discardD
Set.discardc
Make a (shallow) copy of the set.
        There is a 'clone protocol' that subclasses of this class
        should use.  To make a copy, first call your super's _clone()
        method, and use the object returned as the new instance.  Then
        make shallow copies of the attributes defined in the subclass.
        This protocol allows us to write the set algorithms that
        return new instances (e.g. union) once, and keep using them in
        subclasses.
        
_clone_class)
hasattrr
	__class__
__new__r
update)
objr
_cloneJ
Set._clonec
z*Make a (shallow) copy of the set.
        
__copy__`
Set.__copy__c
copyf
Set.copyc
zaUpdate the set, adding any elements from other which are not
        already in the set.
        
other must be a Set instanceN)
isinstancer
otherr
union_updatel
Set.union_updatec
z]Update the set, removing any elements from other which are not
        in both sets.
        r&
intersection_updatex
Set.intersection_updatec
zWUpdate the set, removing any elements from other which are in
        the set.
        r&
clearr
difference_update
Set.difference_updatec
zwReturn a new set which is the union of ``self`` and ``other``.
        Returns the same Set type as this set.
        )
union
z	Set.unionc
Return a new set which is the intersection of ``self`` and
        ``other``.
        Returns the same Set type as this set.
        )
intersection
Set.intersectionc
Return a new set which ``self`` - ``other``, i.e. the items
        in ``self`` which are not also in ``other``.
        Returns the same Set type as this set.
        )

Update the set, adding any elements from other which are not
        already in the set.
        *other*, the collection of items with which to update the set, which
        may be any iterable type.
        N)
